design_philosophy.core_loop: Dig tunnels through maze, inflate or crush enemies with falling rocks, clear stage to progress
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Risk-reward of digging deeper into maze to reach enemies vs. exposure to threats; managing enemy AI pathfinding through solid tiles
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Strategic underground excavator using environment and tools to outsmart creatures
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Emphasizes player agency through environmental manipulation rather than direct combat; marketing positioned as 'strategic digging game' contrasting with Pac-Man's pure maze chase; allows creative problem-solving via rock crushing combos for bonus points

difficulty.lives_system: Multiple lives awarded throughout game; lost on enemy contact, Fygar fire, or rock crush
difficulty.checkpointing: Per-stage progression; 255 stages total with increasing difficulty through enemy speed and quantity
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High - player controls when to engage enemies, which tools to use (pump vs. rock), and route through maze; enemy behavior deterministic but can be manipulated

progression.upgrade_system: Bonus items appear for dropping pairs of rocks; point values increase as player advances (scaling reward system)
progression.unlock_mechanism: Clear all enemies in stage to progress; later stages vary dirt color while escalating enemy count and speed
replayability_hooks: Combo scoring system rewards creative rock-crushing sequences
Increasing enemy aggression and speed creates escalating challenge curve
Strategic maze routing offers multiple valid solutions per stage

world_building_techniques: Character design (Pookas, Fygars) implies fantasy underground setting
Progressive dirt color variation across stages suggests narrative passage

danger_signals: Enemy transformation to ghostly eyes when moving through solid tiles - vulnerability state but increased unpredictability
Falling rocks that can crush player
Last enemy attempting escape; final enemy aggression surge
environmental_storytelling: Underground excavation setting with progressive dirt degradation
Rock physics and environmental hazards as core mechanic
Enemy pathfinding behavior suggests sentience and adaptation

sources_used: wikipedia - Dig Dug article (gameplay mechanics, design philosophy, progression structure, difficulty systems)
